classification_rationale: Vendor's AI is used exclusively for productivity assistance (sentence completion in internal notes) with no decisioning role and no customer-facing exposure. Disclosed features are human-initiated and human-confirmed throughout. Vendor states no PII processing in AI context and no customer-content retention beyond a 24-hour sync cache. SOC 2 Type II attestation current. The use case sits at the productivity-only floor of the risk taxonomy and does not implicate the regulated-decision provisions of OSFI E-23 or NIST AI RMF's high-risk categories.

evidence_cited.reasoning: Reported as 'productivity_only', the lowest tier in the AI usage taxonomy. Places the vendor at the Tier 1 floor per docs/phase-0/01-risk-classification.md.
Meeting note autocomplete is 'human_initiated' (the user types and the model suggests completions in response). No autonomous action; no decisioning.
Smart formatting is 'none' for decision role: it applies markdown to plain text, no semantic decision.

Vendor explicitly states no PII processing. Combined with the productivity-only scope, this removes the primary risk drivers (data residency exposure, model training on customer content) that would elevate the tier.
Current SOC 2 Type II attestation (dated 2025-08-15, 12 month validity) satisfies the baseline security control attestation expected for Tier 1 SaaS vendors.
